content: Just as the lexical definition of "reflexion" in high school English curricula frames it as both a passive physical reflection of light and an active deliberate cognitive practice of reviewing one's own writing, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate both modes: a passive "reflection" of inconsistencies across generations (as seen in Self-Contrast's inconsistent solving perspectives) and an active, structured "reflection" step that explicitly trains the model to align outputs with desired reasoning patterns. This re-framing unlocks a new design axis for LLM reflection that blends implicit inconsistency detection and explicit cognitive review.
